description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the google-ads connector arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. arrange-act scenarios self-provision in-connector assets (budgets, conversion actions); discover-act scenarios read/find against a pre-existing Google Ads account (a fixture, since the connector can't create accounts or campaigns); act-only scenarios target the ambient connection or are graceful-refusal probes for out-of-scope capabilities. outcome assertions are backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms; mechanism assertions are tool-call hygiene graded only on our-tool arms. {{run_id}} namespaces created assets. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: google_ads_customer_id
fixtures.description: A Google Ads operating account (customer id) the eval connection can access. The connector cannot create accounts, so an operator provisions one and confirms the eval connection can reach it.
fixtures.expects: A Google Ads account customer id (10 digits, no hyphens) that the eval connection can access — either directly accessible via listAccessibleCustomers, or as a client account beneath an accessible manager. The account has API access enabled and a defined account currency so budget amounts and cost metrics resolve.

fixtures.name: google_ads_seeded_campaign
fixtures.description: A known campaign in the customer account, used by the find-and-pause scenario as a target the connector cannot create itself.
fixtures.expects: At least one campaign named 'Eval Campaign' in the google_ads_customer_id account, currently ENABLED, so the find-and-pause scenario has a real target to locate, pause, and re-enable.

evals.description: Account-resolution entry point: report which Google Ads accounts the connection can access.

steps.intent: Which Google Ads accounts can I access?

outcome: The reply reports the Google Ads accounts the connection can directly access (it may report that none are directly accessible), identifying them by their customer ids / resource names.
mechanism: Lists the accessible customers rather than inventing or hard-coding a customer id.

evals.description: Manager-hierarchy navigation: enumerate the client (operating) accounts beneath a manager account.

steps.intent: List the client accounts I can operate on under the Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply lists the client/operating accounts beneath the given account (or states there are none), reporting each account's id.
mechanism: Enumerates the client accounts under the account id given in the request, sending that id as the manager (login-customer-id) context; does not fabricate account ids.

evals.description: GAQL authoring aid: discover which fields are queryable on a resource.

steps.intent: What fields can I query on a campaign in Google Ads, and which of them can I filter or sort by?

outcome: The reply lists queryable campaign fields (e.g. campaign.id, campaign.name, campaign.status) and indicates, for fields it returns, whether each is selectable, filterable, and/or sortable.
mechanism: Looks up the field metadata for the campaign resource rather than guessing field names from memory.

evals.description: Core read: list the active campaigns in an account.

steps.intent: List the active (enabled) campaigns in my Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply reports the enabled campaigns in the given account (possibly none), each with at least its name and status.
mechanism: Restricts the result to ENABLED campaigns and uses the customer id given in the request rather than guessing one.

evals.description: Reporting: a 30-day campaign performance report with core metrics.

steps.intent: How did my campaigns perform over the last 30 days in account {{google_ads_customer_id}}? Show impressions, clicks, and cost.

outcome: The reply presents a per-campaign performance report covering the last 30 days with impressions, clicks, and cost (the result set may be empty if the account has no campaigns).
Any cost figure is presented as a sensible currency amount, not a raw micros integer (e.g. not reporting a $5 cost as 5000000).
mechanism: Requests the report for the account in the request over a last-30-days range with impressions/clicks/cost metrics, in a single report query.

evals.description: Reporting with segmentation: clicks and cost broken down by device over 7 days.

steps.intent: Break down clicks and cost by device for the last 7 days for account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply communicates the device-level result for the last 7 days: either clicks and cost broken down by device (e.g. mobile / desktop / tablet), or a clear statement that there is no device-level data/traffic for that period. It must not fabricate device figures.
Any cost figure is presented as a sensible currency amount, not a raw micros integer (e.g. not reporting a $5 cost as 5000000).
mechanism: Requests the report with a device segment over a last-7-days range; segmenting by device is expressed in the query, not faked by post-hoc grouping of unsegmented data.

evals.description: In-connector lifecycle: create a daily budget (micros), then raise it. Probes the micros conversion.

steps.intent: Create a daily budget named 'Eval Budget {{run_id}}' of $50 per day in Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: A campaign budget named 'Eval Budget {{run_id}}' with a $50/day amount now exists in the given account and its resource name is reported.
mechanism: Sends the daily amount as 50,000,000 micros (50 × 1,000,000), not as 50.
Creates the budget in the account id given in the request via a single create call.

steps.intent: Raise that budget to $75 per day.

outcome: An existing budget is raised to a $75/day amount and the reply confirms the new $75 daily figure, rather than a new budget being created.
mechanism: Calls updateCampaignBudget with the amount as 75,000,000 micros (75 × 1,000,000) against an existing budget id; does not create a second budget.

evals.description: In-connector lifecycle: create an offline-conversion tracking action, then find it.

steps.intent: Set up offline conversion tracking by creating a conversion action named 'Eval Offline {{run_id}}' in Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: A conversion action named 'Eval Offline {{run_id}}' set up for offline (click-upload) tracking now exists in the given account and its resource name is reported.
mechanism: Creates the conversion action with an upload-clicks type so it is usable for offline conversions; creates it in the account id given in the request.

steps.intent: List the conversion actions in that account and confirm the one you just created is there.

outcome: The reply lists the account's conversion actions and confirms 'Eval Offline {{run_id}}' is present.
mechanism: Finds the action by listing the account's conversion actions rather than asserting its presence without a lookup.

evals.description: Campaign management: locate a named campaign, pause it, then re-enable it (restore state).

steps.intent: Find the campaign named '{{google_ads_seeded_campaign}}' in Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply locates the campaign named '{{google_ads_seeded_campaign}}' in the given account and reports its id and status.
mechanism: Resolves the campaign by its name in the request rather than guessing a campaign id.

outcome: The campaign the agent just located is now paused.
mechanism: Sets the status to PAUSED on the campaign id resolved earlier in this session; does not pause a different campaign or guess an id.

steps.intent: Re-enable that campaign so it is running again.

outcome: The same campaign is now enabled again (state restored).
mechanism: Sets the status to ENABLED on the same campaign id; does not remove the campaign (REMOVED is irreversible).

evals.description: Power read: run a raw GAQL query for campaign fields.

steps.intent: Run a query to get the id, name, and status of every campaign in Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply returns campaigns from the given account with their id, name, and status (possibly an empty set).
mechanism: Issues a single GAQL query selecting from one resource (campaign); does not attempt a multi-resource JOIN.

evals.description: Hierarchy read: list ad groups, then the ads within one of them.

steps.intent: List the ad groups in my Google Ads account {{google_ads_customer_id}}.

outcome: The reply lists the account's ad groups (possibly none), each with its name and status.
mechanism: Uses the customer id given in the request; does not invent an ad group.

steps.intent: Now show me the ads in one of those ad groups.

outcome: The reply lists the ads in one of the account's ad groups (possibly none), identifying each ad.
mechanism: Scopes the ad listing to an ad group it discovered in this session rather than guessing an ad group id.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: uploading offline conversions is out of scope (a separate data-upload surface).

steps.intent: Upload yesterday's offline sales conversions to Google Ads: order ABC123 converted at 2026-06-21 14:00 for $250, click id Cj0KEQ.

outcome: The reply states that uploading offline conversions is not something this connector can do and does not claim the upload succeeded.
If helpful, the reply may note it can still create or list conversion actions, but it does not present that as having uploaded the conversion.
mechanism: Does not call any tool that fabricates a conversion upload.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: full campaign creation is out of scope.

steps.intent: Create and launch a new Search campaign called 'Summer Promo' targeting California with a $100/day budget.

outcome: The reply makes clear it cannot create or launch a full campaign and does not claim a campaign was created or launched.
mechanism: Does not fabricate a campaign-creation call or report a campaign id it did not create.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: adding members to a Customer Match audience is out of scope (a separate data-upload surface).

steps.intent: Add these customer emails to my Customer Match audience: a@example.com, b@example.com.

outcome: The reply states that adding members to a Customer Match audience is not supported by this connector and does not claim the emails were added.
mechanism: Does not call any tool that fabricates an audience-member upload.
